text:
In a small, sunlit garden filled with pebbles, nestled between two gnarled oaks, lived a whimsical toad named Tortoisesaurus. Tortoisesaurus had two long, warty toes, capable of propelling him through the mud with surprising speed, and an equally long tongue that could slurp up nectar from flowers twice his size. Every morning, he would hop to the edge of his pebble bed and dance around, casting a soft glow onto the ripples he created.

One sunny spring day, a squirrel glided down the fence, its eyes wide at the sight of Tortoisesaurus. Moved by curiosity, the squirrel leapt onto a nearby branch.

"Good morning!" chirped the squirrel, saluting with a paw.

"Ho ho! Good morning to you, little friend!" Tortoisesaurus replied cheerfuly, his laughter creating more ripples on the pond beside him.
